mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion regimes, with a negative or zero Rank IC over consecutive 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months, negating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with a win rate below 45% across all historical trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days during growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to precede positive 20-day returns in high-growth regimes, with forward returns averaging negative or zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal improving manufacturing activity and supply chain throughput, consistently predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.
